title: lachnagrostis striata
intro: Lachnagrostis striata, also known as striped grass or striped blue oat grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the genus Lachnagrostis, native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs and sore throats, and promote healing of wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Lachnagrostis striata is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices that are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
